product: Cisco Meraki Dashboard API v1 — organization action batches

auth.note: required on every request; never emitted in error text

limits.client_rule: split action lists into documented-size chunks preserving order; submit sequentially

body.confirmed: boolean; true for immediate execution (unconfirmed batches sit in preview and are deleted after a week)
body.synchronous: boolean; true forces synchronous execution (max 20 actions)
body.actions: array of {resource, operation, body}; the body key is OMITTED when an action has no body

success.body: {id, organizationId, confirmed, synchronous, status: {completed, failed, errors[], createdResources[{id, uri}]}, actions}
same shape as create; poll until status.completed or status.failed

polling.rule: synchronous batches return a terminal status immediately; asynchronous batches must be polled via get_action_batch until completed or failed, with injected pacing and a hard poll bound

error_correlation.rule: status.errors is an array of strings; each string names the resource path of the failing action, so errors correlate to the action in the submitted batch whose resource appears in the message (index within that batch)
error_correlation.partial_failure: when a later batch fails, successes (created resources, completed counts) from earlier batches are preserved in the report and no further batches are submitted

errors: API rate limit exceeded for organization

rate_limit.client_rule: wait exactly Retry-After via the injected sleep, count the throttle, retry bounded

errors.client_rule: decode into APIError{Status, Errors}; never include the API key
